cases.downtrodden: oppressed or treated badly by those in power
cases.dalliance: a brief casual romantic or sexual relationship
cases.instigate: to bring about or initiate an action or event, often trouble
cases.damper: something that discourages or reduces enthusiasm
cases.dexterity: skill in performing tasks, especially with the hands
cases.bode: to be an omen of a particular outcome, especially a bad one
cases.poultice: a soft moist mass applied to the body to relieve soreness
cases.salvo: a simultaneous discharge of weapons, or a burst of criticism or praise
cases.pincer attack: a military maneuver attacking from both sides at once
cases.herculean: requiring great strength or effort, extremely difficult
cases.bristle: to show sudden irritation or anger
cases.charade: an absurd pretense intended to create a pleasant or respectable impression
cases.wunderkind: a person who achieves great success at a young age, a prodigy
cases.don: to put on an item of clothing
cases.annul: to declare something invalid, especially a law or marriage
cases.slander: a false spoken statement that damages someone's reputation
cases.pratfall: an embarrassing mistake or humiliating failure
cases.lofty: having an imposing height, or noble and elevated in character
cases.tirade: a long angry speech of criticism or accusation
cases.rickety: shaky and likely to collapse, unstable
cases.magnanimous: generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival
cases.chastise: to rebuke or reprimand someone severely
cases.fester: to become worse over time due to being ignored, or to rot
cases.blabbermouth: a person who talks too much, especially revealing secrets
cases.self-aggrandizement: the act of enhancing one's own power or status
cases.drawl: to speak slowly with elongated vowel sounds
cases.lackadaisical: lacking enthusiasm or determination, lazily indifferent
cases.smorgasbord: a wide range of assorted things, originally a buffet of dishes
cases.penny-pincher: a person who is very reluctant to spend money
cases.naivete: a lack of experience, wisdom, or judgment
cases.prostrate: lying flat on the ground, face downward, especially in submission
cases.tantalize: to torment someone with the sight of something desirable but out of reach
cases.self-immolation: the act of killing oneself, especially by fire, as a form of protest
cases.insatiable: impossible to satisfy, having an endless appetite
cases.rabble: a disorganized or disorderly crowd of people, the masses
cases.staunch: loyal and committed in attitude, steadfast
cases.cursory: hasty and without attention to detail
cases.enfant terrible: a person whose unconventional behavior startles or embarrasses others
cases.quibble: a minor objection or complaint about a trivial matter
cases.tight-lipped: reluctant to speak or reveal information
cases.bludgeon: to hit someone repeatedly with a heavy object
cases.lynch: to kill someone, especially by hanging, for an alleged offense without a legal trial
cases.acquit: to formally declare someone not guilty of a crime
cases.perjury: the offense of lying under oath
cases.writhe: to twist or squirm in pain or discomfort
cases.impede: to delay or obstruct someone or something
cases.debonair: confident, stylish, and charming, especially of a man
cases.coterie: a small exclusive group of people with shared interests
cases.obstreperous: noisy and difficult to control, unruly
cases.paltry: small or meager in amount, insignificant
cases.hemorrhage: to lose something rapidly and in large quantity
